sections.paragraph:
m. Chald.-

(1) taste, flavour; specially, pleasant. Dan 5:2, בִּטְעֵם חַמְרָא “in the taste of wine,” i.e. in his cups, whilst drinking.

(2) intelligence, reason, Dan 2:14. יְהַב טַעֲמָא to give account, Dan 6:3. שׂוּם טְעֵם עַל to regard any thing, make account of it, Dan 3:12.

(3) sentence, royal edict, Dan 3:10, 12 , 29 . שׂוּם טְעֵם to give forth a mandate, Ezr 4:19, 21 Ezr 4:21 5:3 , Ezr 5:9, 13 Ezr 5:13 6:1 , Ezr 6:7, 13 Ezr 6:13. Used of a cause to be judged, Ezr 5:5. בְּעֵל טְעֵם holder of judicial authority, or rule, a royal prefect, Ezr 4:8, Ezr 4:9, 17 Ezr 4:17.

I.
